name: Hank Mobley
abstract: Henry Mobley (July 7, 1930 – May 30, 1986) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and composer. Mobley was described by Leonard Feather as the "middleweight champion of the tenor saxophone", a metaphor used to describe his tone, that was neither as aggressive as John Coltrane nor as mellow as Lester Young, and his style that was laid-bac…

concerts.label: Miles Davis with Gil Evans and Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to a lot of Blue Note records, Lee Morgan and Hank Mobley and Horace Silver...
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: Me and Hank Mobley was in the group. We played around quite a bit.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I’m going to get Hank Mobley. So all you got to do is, my same rhythm section, Hank Mobley, and advertise ‘Hank Mobley Quintet with Woody Shaw’... By that time the whole band came out
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: I thought it was tragic how many of our great musicians like Bird, like Lee Morgan and Hank Mobley and so many others man, you know Bud Powell, that had tragic life circumstances
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to a recording that I did with Wynton Kelly, Jimmy Cobb and Hank Mobley.

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: When I played – I didn’t know how good they were either, until I played with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
